description:
You are working as a localization engineer.  
    Inside the working directory /home/user/localization there is an existing UTF-8 CSV file named translations.csv that contains four comma-separated columns in the exact order shown below:

        key,en_US,fr_FR,de_DE
        welcome,Welcome,,Willkommen
        bye,Goodbye,Au revoir,Auf Wiedersehen
        thanks,Thank you,,Danke
        hello,Hello,Bonjour,Hallo
        morning,Good morning,,Guten Morgen

    Goal: produce a plain-text report listing every translation key whose French (fr_FR) column is completely empty.

    Requirements for the report:

    1. The report file MUST be created at the exact path /home/user/localization/missing_fr.txt.  
    2. The file MUST contain ONLY the keys (the first column values) whose fr_FR entry is empty.  
    3. Each key MUST appear on its own line and nothing else may be on any line (no surrounding whitespace).  
    4. The keys MUST be arranged in strictly ascending (A–Z) alphabetical order.  
    5. The file MUST end with a single trailing newline character (i.e. the very last byte of the file is a line-feed).  

    The automated tests will:

    • Verify that /home/user/localization/missing_fr.txt exists.  
    • Check that its contents are exactly:

        morning
        thanks
        welcome

      including the final newline and with no additional blank lines or spaces.  

    Only one terminal command is needed to accomplish this; use any shell tools you prefer. No root access is available.
truth:
Initial state created before the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/localization  
       Permissions: 0755, owned by user:user.

    2. File: /home/user/localization/translations.csv  
       Permissions: 0644, owned by user:user.  
       Exact byte contents (including newlines, Unix LF line-endings):

            key,en_US,fr_FR,de_DE\n
            welcome,Welcome,,Willkommen\n
            bye,Goodbye,Au revoir,Auf Wiedersehen\n
            thanks,Thank you,,Danke\n
            hello,Hello,Bonjour,Hallo\n
            morning,Good morning,,Guten Morgen\n

    Expected final artifact after task completion:

    • File: /home/user/localization/missing_fr.txt  
      Permissions: 0644 or more restrictive, owned by user:user.  
      Exact byte contents (including final newline):

            morning\n
            thanks\n
            welcome\n
